description.sourceText: `Provider body shape for status ${status} is missing fields that consumer reads`
`Provider body shape for status ${status} cannot be fully compared against consumer field expectations`
`Consumer reads "${path.join(".")}" on status ${status}, but the provider declares it optional`
`Provider body shape for status ${providerStatus} is missing fields that consumer reads in default branch`
`Consumer reads "${path.join(".")}" on default branch (status ${providerStatus}), but the provider declares it optional`
`Consumer reads fields from status ${status} response that the declared contract does not include — consumer depends on undeclared implementation details`
`Cannot determine whether consumer's field expectations for status ${status} are covered by the declared contract`
`Consumer expects status ${status}; provider has an opaque status code so satisfaction cannot be verified`
`Consumer expects status ${status} but provider never produces it`
`Contract declares response ${declared} but consumer does not handle it`
`Consumer handles status ${expected} but contract does not declare it`
`Handler transition ${pt.id} returns a body shape incompatible with the declared schema for status ${declared.statusCode}`
`Handler transition ${pt.id} body shape cannot be compared to the declared schema for status ${declared.statusCode}`

description.sourceText: `Provider transition ${pt.id} has an opaque status code; coverage cannot be verified`
`Provider produces status ${status} but no consumer branch handles it`
`Provider has ${providerTransitions.length} distinct cases for status ${status} but consumer does not distinguish between them (transition ${pt.id} has conditions the consumer ignores)`
`Provider transition ${pt.id} for status ${status} has conditions that cannot be verified against consumer predicates`
`Provider transition ${pt.id} for status ${status} produces body with ${formatPath(lit.path)} = ${JSON.stringify(lit.value)}, but no consumer branch tests for this value`
`Provider transition ${pt.id} for status ${status} has body field ${formatPath(field.path)} that other transitions lack, but no consumer branch tests for this field`

value.value: broad-scope rule must constrain at least one field (kind, boundary, or consumer)
narrow-scope rule must specify kind AND (boundary OR consumer.transitionId); set scope: 'broad' to silence wider categories

`Consumer expects status ${status}; provider has an opaque status code so satisfaction cannot be verified`
